jobs.title: Project & Regulatory Toxicologist
jobs.description:
Regulatory Toxicologist (Project)
Principal Scientist OR AD-level
Comp 165- 210k

San Diego Local Candidates Only...no relocation

Job Summary
The Project and Regulatory Toxicologist will be expected to use his/her toxicology expertise to guide safety assessment, design, and interpret in vitro/in vivo studies to evaluate the safety profiles of our covalently and non-covalently targeted small molecules according to regulatory guidelines. You will work closely with the toxicology function, CROs, and multidisciplinary drug discovery/development teams to progress promising therapeutics to the clinic.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities
Represent the toxicology function as a subject matter expert on drug discovery and development project teams, and provide guidance on the safety characterization, prioritization, and advancement of drug candidates
Directly interface with CROs to place appropriate in vitro and in vivo toxicology studies in a timely manner; manage and oversee the execution of toxicology studies at CROs to ensure high-quality data delivery; review and QC study protocols, results, and study reports; summarize toxicology study results and communicate findings to project teams and senior management accurately and punctually
Contribute to the writing of safety study summaries, nonclinical overviews, and other regulatory documents; represent the company at regulatory interactions
Identify and implement new strategies on safety assessment to address current and future safety-related challenges as needed; keep abreast of current regulatory requirements by participating in cross-industry consortia or scientific conferences
Collaborate with early discovery project teams to conduct literature-based target safety assessments and provide guidance on target safety derisking activities

Education and Experience Requirements
Ph.D. in pharmacology, toxicology, or a closely related biomedical discipline with 6+ years of industry experience, or M.S. in the above field with 10+ years of industry experience
Diplomate of the American Board of Toxicology is highly desired
A broad understanding of various aspects of nonclinical safety assessment and regulatory requirements for drug development; experience representing the toxicology function on discovery and development teams is required; expertise in immunology is highly desired
Must be able to critically evaluate, interpret, and integrate multidisciplinary data to support target and compound safety assessment and mechanistic derisking
Experience in the design, conduct, and data interpretation of in vitro and in vivo safety pharmacology, genetic toxicology, and general toxicology studies is required
Experience working with CROs

Required Key Attributes
Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact professionally and effectively in a dynamic, multidisciplinary, and highly collaborative team environment
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Solid problem-solving skills with a strong sense of urgency and scientific curiosity
Proactive and agile, with the ability to operate in a fast-paced work setting
Solution- and result-oriented with attention to detail

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Director

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Science and Research

Industries

Biotechnology Research and Pharmaceutical Manufacturing

jobs.title: Environmental Health Safety Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Description: EHS Specialist

As an EHS (Environmental, Health, and Safety) Specialist at Brothers Desserts you are the champion that focuses our efforts to achieve the safety mission. The right candidate for this role will be a problem solver who influences people and process to ensure we meet our goals and reduce risk to our employees and the environment.
Key Responsibilities

Monitor compliance with OSHA, EPA, FDA, and local regulations
Conduct safety audits, inspections, and risk assessments
Support EHS programs (LOTO, PPE, hazard communication, waste management, etc.)
Investigate incidents, root causes, and implement corrective actions
Support regulatory inspections and third‑party audits
Promote continuous improvement and a strong safety culture by leading Safety committee, SLTs (Safety Learning team)

Required Skills

Advanced understanding of EHS, manufacturing, and regulatory practices
Hazard analysis and risk‑assessment skills
Excellent communication, coaching and training abilities
Incident investigation and root‑cause analysis
Data tracking, report writing, and documentation accuracy
Knowledge of food manufacturing regulations (FDA, FSMA, GMPs)
Ability to work cross‑functionally with production, QA, HR and maintenance teams

Qualifications

2–3 years of EHS experience, preferably in food manufacturing or similar regulated environment
Knowledge of OSHA, EPA, FDA/FSMA, and GMP standards
Strong communication, training, and problem‑solving skills
Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Safety, Industrial Hygiene, or related field (or equivalent experience)
Bilingual Preferred: English/Spanish

Applicable Certifications would include:

OSHA 30 (General Industry or Construction)
Process Safety Management (PSM)
HACCP
Certified Safety Professional (CSP) or Associate Safety Professional (ASP)
First Aid/CPR

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Mid-Senior level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Manufacturing

Industries

Food and Beverage Manufacturing
